programme.title: Pie stūres
programme.desc: Deutsche Welle žurnāls

programme.title: Rīta Panorāma
programme.desc: Enerģisks un iedvesmojošs sākums jaunai dienai! Ieskats gaidāmās dienas notikumos Latvijā un pasaulē, kultūras, izklaides un sporta ziņas, laika prognoze. Arī atskats uz aizvadītās dienas notikumiem, pievēršot uzmanību to attīstībai nakts laikā. Aizrautīgas sarunas ar viesiem un skanīgi muzikālie priekšnesumi. Raidījumu vada Gusts Kikusts, Sigurds Siliņš, Karīna Rubene, Danuta Juste, sporta ziņas lasa Reinis Ošenieks, Raimonds Gekišs, laika ziņas – Ilze Skudra, Ilze Golubeva, Toms Bricis.

programme.title: Šodienas jautājums
programme.desc: No pirmdienas līdz ceturtdienai 20:35 LTV1 demonstrē diskusiju raidījumu "Šodienas jautājums", kurā tiek analizēts un skaidrots kāds no attiecīgās dienas svarīgākajiem notikumiem. Koncentrēti, neieslīgstot liekvārdībā, raidījuma dalībnieki runā tiešām par būtiskāko! Operatīvi reaģējot uz aktuālajiem dienas notikumiem, studijā tiešraidē tiekas dažādu jomu eksperti un politiķi.

programme.title: Ceturtā studija
programme.desc: Raidījuma "4. studija" misija ir palīdzēt risināt sabiedrībai aktuālus ikdienas jautājumus visās dzīves sfērās, kas ietekmē katra indivīda un sabiedrības dzīves kvalitāti. Raidījuma misija formulēta sauklī "Neērti jautājumi ērtākai ikdienai". Raidījums aktualizē problēmas, ar kurām saskaras ikviens Latvijas iedzīvotājs, sākot ar ģimeni, mācību un darba vidi, publisko telpu un beidzot ar dažādu privāto, pašvaldību un valsts iestāžu darbību un to sniegtajiem pakalpojumiem.

programme.title: Aizraujošie vilcienu maršruti
programme.desc: Maikls Portillo sāks Indijas izpētes tūri un šajā sērijā dosies uz valsts ziemeļiem. Ceļojumu viņš sāks Amritsarā, kas ir bēdīgi slavena ar 1919. gada slaktiņu. Maikls tur apmeklēs iespaidīgo Zelta templi un kļūs par palīgu lielākajā bezmaksas virtuvē pasaulē. Tad cauri auglīgiem laukiem Maikls dosies uz Ludhiānu, kur apmeklēs Edītes Braunas dibināto medicīnas skolu, kas nodrošināja labāku nākotni sievietēm, bet Ludhiānas laukos baudīs čapātī plāceni. Ambāla ir nozīmīgs transporta mezgls, kas savieno Ziemeļindijas pilsētas, un te Maikls nolēmis iepirkties un uzcirsties pēc vietējo modes. Nākamā pietura – neparasti tīrā, kārtīgā un Indijas raksturam netipiskā Pendžābas galvaspilsēta Čandīgarha, kur Maikls uzdejos kādu tradicionālu deju. Pēdējā ceļojuma posmā Maikls no Kālkas uz Šimlas kalnu staciju dosies vienā no skaistākajiem vilcienu maršrutiem pasaulē.

programme.title: 1:1. Aktuālā intervija
programme.desc: Raidījumā "1:1" [viens pret vienu] satiekas sabiedrībā zināma personība un žurnālists. Raidījums skaidro, analizē un ļauj izprast sabiedrībai nozīmīgas norises visdažādākajās dzīves jomās Latvijā un pasaulē, izprast personības nozīmīgumu, atbildību šo procesu virzībā. Viesi izvēlēti, ņemot vērā tā brīža aktualitātes vai arī konkrētās personas iespējamo ietekmi uz notikumiem nākotnē, – valsts pirmās amatpersonas, biznesa, kultūras un nevalstiskā sektora pārstāvji. Raidījumu vada žurnālists Gundars Rēders.

programme.title: Literatūre
programme.desc: "Literatūre" ir literārā tūrisma dokumentāls raidījumu cikls, kas apskata latviešu literatūru no sendienām līdz mūsdienām. Katrā epizodē kāds latviešu rakstnieks kā ceļa karti ņem kādu sev tuva aizgājušo laiku autora grāmatu un kopā ar literatūristi Martu dodas ceļojumā uz vietām, kur tas dzīvojis vai par kurām rakstījis. Savukārt, viņu savrupais ceļabiedrs Gustavs ceļo atsevišķi ar grāmatām un meklē, kam palasīt priekšā.

programme.title: TE! Latvijas raidījums
programme.desc: Astoņās raidījuma sērijās, ceļojot laikā caur Latvijas pagātni, tagadni un potenciālo nākotni, ar piedzīvojuma garšu, rotaļīgumu un izteiktu klātbūtnes sajūtu "TE!" vedīs jaunos Latvijas apceļošanas piedzīvojumos. Raidījumu vada Marta Selecka, Dace Salnāja, Māris Olte, Pauls Timrots, Māra Sleja un Gustavs Terzens. Raidījumu veido "VFS Films", un projektu līdzfinansē Mediju atbalsta fonds no Latvijas valsts budžeta līdzekļiem.

programme.title: Alpu dakteris
programme.desc: Nav labākas vietas, kur patverties no pilsētas kņadas, trokšņa un stresa, kā kalni. Bet dzīvot tur? Dzimto Alpu mazpilsētiņu, saucot tās gaisotni par smacējošu un aprobežotu, Mārtins pameta jau pirms daudziem gadiem, lai kļūtu par veiksmīgu ārstu kosmopolītiskajā lielpilsētā Ņujorkā. Nu viņš ir atgriezies. Uz pavisam īsu apciemojumu, lai atkal drīz atvadītos. Taču no likteņa neizbēgsi - atgriešanās mājās izrādās uz palikšanu. Jo pārāk liels ir trieciens, uzzinot, ka viņa pirmā mīlestība, kas pēc Mārtina aizbraukšanas apprecēja viņa paša brāli Hansu, ir mirusi. Taču atstājusi meitu, par kuras esamību Mārtins nekad pat nebija zinājis! Ar to izrādās pietiekami, lai izlemtu palikt Pēkšņi kļuvis par tēvu, Mārtins vēlas uzzināt vairāk par savu jauniegūto meitu Lili, iemantot viņas uzticēšanos. Tā dēļ viņš ir gatavs pat mēģināt sadarboties ar savu jaunāko brāli Hansu, kas joprojām jūt aizvainojumu. Hanss ir kļuvis par glābēju komandas vadītāju, un kopā ar Mārtinu viņi turpmāk neskaitāmas reizes riskēs ar savu dzīvību, lai glābtu kalnu negadījumu upurus vai kāpējus, ko pārsteigusi pēkšņa vētra. Mārtinu gaida atklāsme, ka bez saknēm nav iespējas turpināt dzīvi. Šeit viņš pilnībā aptvers arī to, ka būt par ārstu kalnos - tā nav tikai profesija, bet gan aicinājums. Kļūt par labu neatliekamās medicīniskās palīdzības sniedzēju kalnos iemācīties nevar, par tādu piedzimst. Un Mārtins Grūbers tāds neapšaubāmi ir!

programme.title: Projekts Nākotne
programme.desc: Deutsche Welle žurnāls

programme.title: Zinātnes noslēpumi
programme.desc: Dažādas mūsu ikdienā ierastas darbības un notikumi, izskaidroti no zinātnes viedokļa.

programme.title: Kultūrdeva
programme.desc: Raidījumā "Kultūrdeva" viesosies Latvijas Nacionālās operas un baleta vadītājs Sandis Voldiņš, kurš pastāstīs par Rīgas Operas festivālu, kā arī 20 gadu jubileju atzīmējošās "Dabas koncertzāles" veidotāji Ingus Ulmanis un Andris Sējāns, kuri stāstīs par gatavošanos uzvedumam "Invasivus!", kas notiks bijušās Slokas papīrfabrikas teritorijā.

programme.title: Montija Dona Reinzemes dārzi
programme.desc: Šoreiz Montijs Dons seko Reinai Vācijas vēsturiskajās pilsētās, pilīs un vīndārziem klātajās ielejās, tad nonāk valsts rūpnieciskajā centrā un atklāj, kā upe savā ceļā turpina veidot cilvēku dzīvi, ainavu un dārzkopības kultūru. Švecingenē Montijs uzzina, ka Reina tika pārveidota, lai pa to būtu vieglāk kuģot lieliem ūdens transportlīdzekļiem, taču šo pārmaiņu sekas ir jūtamas apkārtnes dārzos un parkos. Montijs viesojas arī botāniskajā dārzā Frankfurtē, vāciešu iecienītajos mazdārziņos, kā arī apmeklē pirmā Rietumvācijas kanclera Konrāda Adenauera dārzu.

programme.title: Komisārs Reksis
programme.desc: Davide ar Reksi skatās nacionālās peldēšanas sacensības, kad tās pārtrauc šokējošs notikums – sacensību līderis un Itālijas peldēšanas uzlecošā zvaigzne mirst. Nekavējoties tiek uzsākta izmeklēšana, un ir jānoskaidro, kāda loma sportista nāvē ir bijusi Gabrielem, viņa sīvākajam konkurentam sportā un privātajā dzīvē. Tajā pašā laikā rodas jautājumi par peldētāja ārkārtīgi stingrā tēva attiecībām ar dēlu, kā arī sportista draudzenes lomu notikušajā.

programme.title: Aizraujošie vilcienu maršruti
programme.desc: Šajā sērijā vilciens vedīs Maiklu Portillo no Taras tuksneša Rādžastānas štatā līdz Indijas galvaspilsētai, ļaujot gan izbaudīt tuksneša ainavas, gan apskatīt satriecošu arhitektūru tā sauktajā "Zelta trijstūra" maršrutā. Džajpurā Maikls uzzinās par draudzību britu un indiešu karalisko ģimeņu vidū un spēlēs polo ar maharadžu. Āgrā viņš apbrīnos vareno Tadžmahālu un iepazīs tā rašanās vēsturi, savukārt Deli mūsdienu ceļotājs izbaudīs lielisku vietējo ēdienu, kā arī izbrauks ar pavisam jauno pilsētas metro un savām acīm redzēs Indijas vēsturi Ņūdeli – pilsētā, kuru britu arhitekts projektēja britiem, bet kura nu ir demokrātiskās Indijas galvaspilsēta.

programme.title: Policijas iecirknis
programme.desc: Atklājas, ka bezpajumtnieku nolaupīšana un pazušana ir saistīta ar daudz nopietnāku organizāciju, kas nodarbojas ar orgānu kontrabandu. Nošautā suņa lietā tiek atklāti vēl trīs upuri – šoreiz tie ir cilvēku līķi. Tikmēr Patrika paziņam Paskālam un viņa draudzenei Valērijai izsolē trāpās kas negaidīts.

programme.title: Vetārsts Jākobss
programme.desc: Švānicā, mazā ziemeļu ciemā pie Baltijas jūras, ierodas Hauke Jākobss, kurš tur grib atvērt veterināro klīniku. Tas visvairāk iepriecina agrākā veterinārārsta asistenti Jūli Kristianzeni, kura baidās palikt bez darba un dzīvesvietas. Arī vietējo policisti Lonu Fogtu ieinteresē jaunais ciema iedzīvotājs, kad viņa uzzina, ka Hauke agrāk strādājis Hamburgas policijā. Taču Hauke izvairās no uzmanības un nelabprāt runā par savu pagātni, dodot priekšroku izolētai dzīvei uz kutera kopā ar suni Holiju.

programme.title: Dzīvei nav melnraksta
programme.desc: "Mamma, lūdzu, lūdzu, apsoli man, ka tu neko ar sevi nenodarīsi." Un es pateicu: "Meitiņ, es tev apsolu." Tas bija tas mirklis, kad es sapratu, ka man kaut kādā veidā ir jādzīvo. Es apsolīju savam bērnam, un es sapratu, ka nevaru to nodarīt saviem bērniem, lai cik man grūti būtu bijis," rubrikā "Dzīvei nav melnraksta" stāsta Marika Liekmane. Marika ilgu laiku sevi uztvēra kā emocionāli stipru cilvēku - tādu, kas "tiek galā pati". Viņa neuzskatīja, ka viņai būtu mentālās veselības problēmas. Lūzuma punkts bija brīdis, kad ārsti viņai paziņoja par iespējamu onkoloģisku slimību, kas vēlāk apstiprinājās. Šī diagnoze kļuva par pirmo smago triecienu - sajūtu, ka dzīve vienā dienā var sabrukt. Tomēr izšķirošais brīdis, kas viņu salauza, bija personīgs zaudējums - cilvēks, kuram viņa bija emocionāli pieķērusies, pēkšņi pazuda no viņas dzīves bez paskaidrojuma. Vissmagākās bija domas: vēlme vienkārši aizmigt un nekad nepamosties.

programme.title: Dzīvei nav melnraksta
programme.desc: "Negribas, lai paliek sliktāk. Negribas, lai viņai atsakās rociņas un kājiņas. Es negribu, lai viņai sāp, tāpēc ļoti gribētos atrast zāles," rubrikā "Dzīvei nav melnraksta" stāsta Sintija Bergmane. Sintija meitai meklēja diagnozi, jo saprata, ka kaut kas ar bērnu nav kārtībā. Viņa neattīstījās atbilstoši savam vecumam, bieži slimoja, bija krampji. Apmeklēja daudzus ārstus, bet neviens īsti nespēja palīdzēt. Diagnozi varēja uzstādīt vien 9 gadu vecumā. Tā izrādījās ļoti reta ģenētiska slimība, kuras ārstēšanai zāļu pasaulē nav.

programme.title: Cīņa par varu izplatījumā
programme.desc: Kopš 1957. gada, kad Padomju Savienība palaida orbītā pirmo Zemes mākslīgo pavadoni "Sputņik", kosmosā ir nogādāti vairāk nekā 10 000 satelītu. Veselas ekonomikas nozares - no telekomunikācijas un transporta līdz finansēm un meteoroloģijai - balstās uz satelītu pārraidīto informāciju. Kosmosā atspoguļojas arī Zemes ģeopolitiskā situācija. Aukstā kara laikā sākās sacensība starp ASV un Krieviju, bet tagad jaunu kosmosa lielvaru, piemēram, Ķīnas un Indijas, kompetenču pieaugums maina spēles noteikumus. Tehnoloģiju attīstība, kas ļauj iznīcināt satelītus no Zemes, spiegošanas satelītu izvietošana, kiberuzbrukumu risks... Vai mēs esam uz zvaigžņu karu sliekšņa?

programme.title: Projekts Nākotne
programme.desc: Deutsche Welle žurnāls

programme.title: Uz Tobago. Kurzemniekiem pa pēdām
programme.desc: Tobago – šīs Karību jūras salas pieminēšana latvieša iztēlē uzbur ainas ar kurzemnieku kuģiem, kas vētru plosīti ar pēdējiem spēkiem piestāj mežonīgas cilvēkēdāju cilts apdzīvotas salas krastā. Tobago veido arī daļiņu no katra latvieša pašapziņas un lepnuma par kurzemnieku drosmi un piedzīvojumu garu – pašiem sava kolonija Jaunkurzeme! Šo pasaules jaunatklājēju garu uzburs piedzīvojumu ekspedīcijas "Uz Tobago. Kurzemniekiem pa pēdām" sešas sērijas kopā ar Sandru Kropu, kura meklēs kurzemnieku atstātās liecības mūsdienu Tobago. Sandra nirs vairāk kā 20 metru dziļumā Karību jūras dzelmē, lai apskatītu bruņurupuču slepeno dzīvi, dosies pārsteigumiem pilnos džungļos, ar vietējiem zvejniekiem, saulei austot, vilks lomu, iepazīs vietējo virtuvi, piedalīsies vergu laiku kāzu rituālā, baudīs kaismīgas naktis dzelzs bungu rīboņas pavadībā, ievāks mango ražu un, iepazīstot krāšņās Karību salas iedzīvotājus, dabu un tradīcijas, soli pa solim šķetinās kurzemnieku laika noslēpumus. Un to vēl ir atliku likām...

programme.title: Uz Tobago. Kurzemniekiem pa pēdām
programme.desc: Tobago – šīs Karību jūras salas pieminēšana latvieša iztēlē uzbur ainas ar kurzemnieku kuģiem, kas vētru plosīti ar pēdējiem spēkiem piestāj mežonīgas cilvēkēdāju cilts apdzīvotas salas krastā. programme.title: Aizliegtais paņēmiens
programme.desc: Lai uzzinātu par kāda notikuma patiesajiem apstākļiem vai iesaistīto personu nodomiem, raidījuma "Aizliegtais paņēmiens" komanda informācijas iegūšanai izmanto visdažādākos izmeklējošās žurnālistikas paņēmienus un žanrus – eksperimentus, testus, provokācijas, padziļinātus pētījumus, arī anonīmas intervijas. Žurnālisti padziļināti pēta un skatītājiem atklāj sociālpolitiskus tematus, kas ne vienmēr tiek izcelti citos medijos. Raidījums atklāj un atmasko, atsevišķos gadījumos sauc pie atbildības iesaistītās personas, norāda uz likumu nepilnībām, aicina mainīt pastāvošo kārtību, ja tā ir pretrunā sabiedrības interesēm. Raidījumu vada LTV Ziņu dienesta žurnālists Guntis Bojārs.

programme.title: Kas notiek Latvijā?
programme.desc: Diskusijas un intervijas par sabiedrībai nozīmīgām norisēm. Vada žurnālists Jānis Domburs.

programme.title: Pasaules čempionāts hokejā. Ungārija – Latvija
programme.desc: Ar Ungāriju Latvija spēlējusi pavisam reti, un nekad mūsdienās tas nav noticis pasaules čempionātos. Ir tikai divas savstarpējās spēles olimpiskajā kvalifikācijas turnīrā, kad Latvija svinējusi pārliecinošas uzvaras ar 9 pret 0 un 7 pret 3. Tas gan bija 2009. un 2021. gadā, tātad pirmā reize bija visai sen. Mūsējiem jāspēlē ar pinu atdevi, mūsdienās neviena komanda tāpat vien nepadodas, un arī mazāk pieredzējušas komandas tagad bieži vien visai sīksti pretojas spēcīgākām komandām. Ir jānoskaņojas spēlei, turklāt tā notiks pa dienu, kas ir diezgan neparasti. Latvijas sastāvs ir krietni atjaunināts, šopavasar izlasē nebūs arī ilggadējā kapteiņa Kaspara Daugaviņa, un jācer, ka mūsējie visas spēles spēs aizvadīt ātrā tempā, kas var radīt papildu slodzi pretiniekiem.

programme.title: 1:1. Aktuālā intervija
programme.desc: Raidījumā "1:1" [viens pret vienu] satiekas sabiedrībā zināma personība un žurnālists. Raidījums skaidro, analizē un ļauj izprast sabiedrībai nozīmīgas norises visdažādākajās dzīves jomās Latvijā un pasaulē, izprast personības nozīmīgumu, atbildību šo procesu virzībā. Viesi izvēlēti, ņemot vērā tā brīža aktualitātes vai arī konkrētās personas iespējamo ietekmi uz notikumiem nākotnē, – valsts pirmās amatpersonas, biznesa, kultūras un nevalstiskā sektora pārstāvji. Raidījumu vada žurnālists Gundars Rēders.

programme.title: Lielās patiesības
programme.desc: "Savienotie trauki" - tā saucas LU profesora, vēsturnieka Kaspara Kļaviņa grāmata, kurā viņš aplūko sākumā šķietami neredzamu saistību starp Austrumu un Rietumu kultūrām. Sarunā ar raidījuma vadītāju Ilmāru Latkovski profesors Kļaviņš dalās savā pieredzē un vērojumos, kas gūti, strādājot Dienvidkorejas, Ķīnas u. c. universitātēs. Saruna par atšķirībām diskusiju kultūtrā, par spēju atšķirt būtisko no nebūtiskā. Cik daudz mūsdienu pasaules konflikti atbilst Semjuela Hantingtona teorijai par civilizāciju sadursmi.

programme.title: Incas: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: In less than a century, the Incas built monumental remains throughout their vast empire, but the secrets of their lightning-fast expansion remained unknown for centuries. Under the conqueror Tupac Yupanqui, the empire grew through force and clever alliances. He led the greatest territorial expansion of the Inca realm. He subdued the Incas' fiercest rivals along the Pacific coast. From Cuzco, the heart of his empire, he also established a vast imperial administration, supported by a 40,000-kilometer road network, custom-built administrative centers, and a curious accounting system based on knotted strings. Thanks to the latest scientific advances and ultra-realistic 3D reconstructions from Cuzco to the Atacama Desert, via the Pacific coast, this is the lightning expansion of the Empire of the Sun.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Making a routine journey from Berlin, Crossair Flight #3597 is on its final approach to Zurich. It is one of the last planes scheduled to land. But just minutes before landing the pilots cannot find the runway. They decide to abort the maneuver and apply full power to all four engines in order to get their jet to climb. But they are too late. The aircraft clips the treetops below and crashes into a hillside. Investigators comb through the wreckage, and the data from the plane's black boxes. But they would only solve the riddle of Crossair Flight 3597 by digging into the past.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: Facing a spate of thunderstorms, Margot makes one final, all out push to complete her Yukon dream home. Billy builds a backwoods BBQ - the last step in his goal to turn his simple abode into the perfect wilderness homestead. With summer right around the corner, Matty takes steps to "up" his waterfront "game". And Emilie and Gilbert battle late season snows as they rush to complete their backcountry cabin before some very special guests arrive.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: With warmer weather just around the corner, Margot and Alex hit the trail to begin their summer horse guiding season. Billy gives his newly refurbished boat the ultimate sea-worthiness test on the mighty St. Lawrence River. Matty and Kelly take another step toward complete self-sufficiency with the addition of some "ovine" friends, and despite late-season snow, Emilie and Gilbert redouble their efforts to complete a family-friendly hunting camp.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: To maintain its place as one of the busiest airports in the world, Dubai International needs to run like clockwork. Failure in any single department can have major consequences across the whole airport. This episode follows an emergency in air traffic control that leads to a runway closure, a huge task to re-number every departure gate in the airport while it is operational, and the mounting tension on the building site of a new $3.2 billion terminal extension - built specifically to accommodate the A380, the world's biggest passenger airliner.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: In this episode, an incoming aircraft with a hydraulics problem threatens to bring every flight to a halt; 40,000 thousand feet above a virtual Dubai, two trainee A380 pilots simulate an almost identical emergency; an inspection of the upgraded Southern runway delays the re-opening - bad news for the airport's billion dollar engineering programme and recording artist Cat Stevens is at risk of missing a crucial flight to New York to be inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, all because of a computer problem at the Departure Gate.

programme.title: Incas: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: The Inca Empire rose in the 15th century, yet its origins remained shrouded in mystery for centuries. Follow Manco Capac, the first Inca and Pachacutec, the mythical emperor who reshaped a civilization. Pachacutec built Machu Picchu, revolutionized architecture, and drew on the legacy of an older society to rewrite his people's history. Claiming the title "Son of the Sun," he expanded the empire beyond the Cuzco Valley, imposed a solar cult as the state religion, and even instituted human sacrifices to assert his power. With cutting-edge science and ultra-realistic 3D reconstructions from Machu Picchu to Chilean volcanoes, Lake Titicaca, and Cuzco, witness the breathtaking birth of the Empire of the Sun.

programme.title: Incas: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: In less than a century, the Incas built monumental remains throughout their vast empire, but the secrets of their lightning-fast expansion remained unknown for centuries. Under the conqueror Tupac Yupanqui, the empire grew through force and clever alliances. He led the greatest territorial expansion of the Inca realm. He subdued the Incas' fiercest rivals along the Pacific coast. From Cuzco, the heart of his empire, he also established a vast imperial administration, supported by a 40,000-kilometer road network, custom-built administrative centers, and a curious accounting system based on knotted strings. Thanks to the latest scientific advances and ultra-realistic 3D reconstructions from Cuzco to the Atacama Desert, via the Pacific coast, this is the lightning expansion of the Empire of the Sun.

programme.title: Science of Stupid
programme.desc: Science of Stupid, series 8. Dallas Campbell hosts the show that combines serious science with some of the craziest, most spectacular user generated clips ever recorded. In each programme we show 40 to 50 clips of misadventure: ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes and paying the price. We analyse the science behind the mishaps, with the help of bespoke animation and super slow-motion cinematography.

programme.title: Science of Stupid
programme.desc: Science of Stupid, series 8. Dallas Campbell hosts the show that combines serious science with some of the craziest, most spectacular user generated clips ever recorded. In each programme we show 40 to 50 clips of misadventure: ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes and paying the price. We analyse the science behind the mishaps, with the help of bespoke animation and super slow-motion cinematography.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Flight: TAM Airlines Flight 402; Fokker 100; October 31st, 1996. Just seconds after lifting off from Congonhas airport in Sao Paulo, Brazil, TAM Airlines flight 402 slams into a residential neighborhood and bursts into flames killing all 95 people on board. Investigators from the Brazilian Air Force join forces with the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board to uncover a series of events that led to a failure so rare, it seemed impossible.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Harrowing stories of tragedy and triumph are brought to life through official reports, transcripts and interviews with the pilots, air traffic controllers and lucky survivors of history's most terrifying crashes. Widely considered to be the safest form of travel, air transportation is still in its infancy and when midair calamity strikes, the results are often catastrophic. From the cockpit to the cabin, from the control room to the crash scene, we uncover just what went wrong.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster
programme.desc: On 3rd October 1993, US soldiers from the elite units of the U.S. Army's Rangers and Delta Force stormed a building near the in Mogadishu, Somalia, to capture key lieutenants in warlord Mohamed Farrah Aidid's clan. What should have been a simple snatch and grab operation lasting under an hour, turned into a day and night battle that resulted in the deaths of 18 Rangers and an estimated 500 to 1,000 Somali civilians.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster
programme.desc: Over two days in spring of 1996, 8 climbers died on the descent from Mount Everest. A fierce storm, hubris and ambition and a series of errors contributed to make this the deadliest day in the history of the mountain. On May 10th two competing commercial guided expeditions were making the ascent to Everest. Adventure Consultants led by New Zealand climbing veteran Rob Hall and Mountain Madness led by accomplished American climber Scott Fischer led clients, each of whom paid up to $65,000, to summit the peak. Dr Jeremy Windsor a leading expert on altitude will examine the dangers posed by climbing at such a high altitude where oxygen in the air is three times less than sea level, and reveals weather data that may have uniquely contributed to the tragedy

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: China is a land of immense scale and diversity, an ancient civilization with a history dating back thousands of years. How have the traditions of China shaped its landscape and people, and how do they hold up in the present day? To capture its scale and complexity we embark on an epic aerial journey across Chinas varied landscapes and cultures to uncover how these practices and icons endure. From the monumental engineering feats of the Great Wall to a massive water splashing festival in the tropical south, we unveil the secrets and patterns of Chinas living past in a fresh new perspective.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: In episode two, we take to the air to reveal how modern China lives,eats,works,travels andplays on a mega scale. We will learn how this nation has transformed its cities and infrastructure in three decades while still retaining its traditions. We gain a greater understanding of how Chinas industrialization impacts the planet in negative ways, and how they are seeking solutions. And we find out what a billion plus population does for fun and how it crosses the countrys huge expanses.

programme.title: Airport Security: Colombia
programme.desc: Security officials at El Dorado International Airport, in Bogota, Colombia, continue to foil the most audacious attempts at drug trafficking. On this new season of Airport Alert, they must detect sophisiticated new drug smuggling methods, arrest luggage thieves and detain suspects with international arrest warrants, while also dealing with medical emergencies, document forgery and cases of identity theft. Ranked as one of the 23 best airpots in the world, El Dorado is one of the biggest and most dynamic air terminals in Latin America. The airport serves 25 million passengers and handles 200,000 tons of cargo every year in its 1,100-hectare facility. Things are moving day and night, and appearances can be deceiving. Airport Alert follows agents 24/7 and captures their efforts to ensure the security of passengers and cargo alike.

programme.title: Airport Security: Colombia
programme.desc: El Dorado International Airport officials must put their sagacity and experience to the test in order to detect the boldest and most original attempts at breaking the law that take place at the airport. In this episode, three passengers are caught carrying a total of 120 cocaine capsules inside their bodies and they in turn confess the dramatic circumstances they claim pushed them into committing a crime. Immigration agents face the challenge of deporting eight Bangladeshi nationals who are traveling using false documentation. Also, a young man shows great imagination when he hides a stash of cocaine in an unexpected part of his luggage. And a Dutch passenger dares to carry large quantities of cocaine in the fake bottom of both his suitcases. Meanwhile, customs officials work tirelessly to stop undeclared cash from entering the country, as it is sometimes linked to criminal activity.

programme.title: Airport Security: Peru and Brazil
programme.desc: The intelligence team of the Federal Police has detected two passengers who could be traveling with drugs, but thanks to the work of the officers, they manage to intercept four suspects. Meanwhile, immigration agents must use all their imagination to communicate and gain the trust of a group of passengers who are apparently trying to enter the country irregularly. For their part, the customs agents must prevent counterfeit merchandise from entering the country.

programme.title: Amsterdam Central 24/7
programme.desc: Festival season has kicked off in the city, culminating in the Amsterdam Dance Event, the world's largest electronic music festival. Around 500,000 dance enthusiasts from around the world are gathering in Amsterdam to attend the festivities. This means tens of thousands of extra passengers at the station. Safety & Service staff Menno and Baran are busy dealing with visitors who are using drugs excessively. Veteran Menno tries to keep his new colleague Baran in check when he chases a troublemaker who is under the influence. Amsterdam Central is also joining in the festivities, with various stages and a special Dance Train featuring a DJ. On board, conductor Errol is transported back to his time as a party animal.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: February 12th, 2009 - Two hours behind schedule and nearing the end of its 53-minute trip from Newark, New Jersey, Continental Connections Flight #3407 is approaching Buffalo-Niagara International Airport. Flying through snow and strong winds, the pilots can see the lights of the city below, but as they extend the flaps to slow the plane, the control column suddenly starts to shake. It's a warning that the plane is about to stall. The captain struggles to keep the plane flying but the out-of-control aircraft suddenly plummets towards the ground and crashes into a suburban home. It is the worst US crash in more than 7 years, but with a raging fire consuming the wreckage, can investigators determine what went wrong?

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Flight: TAM Airlines Flight 402; Fokker 100; October 31st, 1996. Just seconds after lifting off from Congonhas airport in Sao Paulo, Brazil, TAM Airlines flight 402 slams into a residential neighborhood and bursts into flames killing all 95 people on board. Investigators from the Brazilian Air Force join forces with the U.S. National Transportation Safety Board to uncover a series of events that led to a failure so rare, it seemed impossible.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: Guarded by the Terracotta Warriors, the tomb of the First Emperor is so revered that it has never been opened. But now, for the first time ever, Sam Willis and his team of experts use new laser drone technology to investigate. What they reveal has never been seen before, and it provides new insight into what lies inside the tomb itself, as they piece together the Emperor's vast mausoleum - an entire, stunning city of the dead.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: Dr Allan Maca explores a newly discovered ancient megacity that 4000 years ago was one of the biggest on the planet. Its people created amazing Maya-like carvings like nothing ever found in China before. There's also grizzly evidence of human sacrifice on an unprecedented scale. This lost civilisation changes everything we thought we knew about the origins of China, but why, after 500 years, did they vanish? Allan and his team investigate ancient cataclysms to try to solve this great mystery.

programme.title: Airport Security: Peru and Brazil
programme.desc: This season features four episodes each at two of Latin America's biggest airports: Lima, Peru's Jorge Chavez and Brazil's S?o Paulo-Guarulhos. Police are on high alert. Their main challenges: illegal immigration and the trafficking of cocaine. From hidden compartments and ingested capsules to fake documents, officers work 24/7 to protect their borders and stop criminals in their tracks. Watch the action from behind the scenes.

programme.title: Airport Security: To Catch a Smuggler Latin America Compilation
programme.desc: These carriers are frequent flyers. From Airport Security Colombia and Peru, a Spaniard with cocaine pretends to be shocked. A man going to the DR with cocaine tells officers he has been there before. A man on his second trip claims he was forced. A woman uses someone else's authentic passport to travel. An Indonesian woman carries cocaine and multiple types of currency. A freelance trafficker boasts he has information on a 1000 kilo load.

programme.title: Airport Security: Brazil
programme.desc: Federal Police at Sao Paolo International Airport work 24-7 to stop illegal activity. Their main threat, cocaine smuggling. Agents arrest two different traffickers with bricks of cocaine branded in the same curious way. One of those loads is among the largest of the last few years. Panicked asylum seekers refuse to cooperate, causing an uproar. And suitcases carrying hundreds of counterfeit goods are intercepted.

programme.title: Amsterdam Central 24/7
programme.desc: Amsterdam Central Station is a haven for the homeless. Due to a housing shortage, migration, and long wait times for mental health services, around 100 people hang around the station every day. Field workers Marieke and Fikret try to help people with ID, shelter, or a train ticket home. But that's not easy: many homeless people refuse to be helped. Warehouse worker Ron is at odds with the deposit machines in the station. Law enforcement officers Menno and Baran are dealing with an aggressive can collector. Manager Mike shows off the world's largest underground bicycle parking facility. On the day the so-called "orphan bikes" are collected—bikes that haven't been picked up after a month—he and his team have to step up their efforts. And that's not the only bicycle parking facility; a new one is being built under the railway line.

programme.title: Amsterdam Central 24/7
programme.desc: King's Day is the busiest day of the year at Amsterdam Central Station. While Ticket & Services employee Alexandra, and Station Manager René try to steer the crowds in the right direction, Assistant Manager Michel is busy coordinating the flow of goods due to a broken freight elevator. Meanwhile, officers Menno and Carolien revive a girl who has fallen ill.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: Guarded by the Terracotta Warriors, the tomb of the First Emperor is so revered that it has never been opened. But now, for the first time ever, Sam Willis and his team of experts use new laser drone technology to investigate. What they reveal has never been seen before, and it provides new insight into what lies inside the tomb itself, as they piece together the Emperor's vast mausoleum - an entire, stunning city of the dead.

This lost civilisation changes everything we thought we knew about the origins of China, but why, after 500 years, did they vanish? This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Primal Survivor
programme.desc: Hazen Audel embarks on a challenging jungle mission, based on a traditional solo jungle trek made by young men of the Embera tribe. Hazen will leave behind the safety of the Embera tribal village and journey in to the heart of one of the world's most hostile and challenging environments - the rainforests of the notorious Darien Gap. On his multi-day challenge Hazen must cross rivers, dark jungles and scale mountains if he is to reach his final destination of the Pacific coast.

programme.title: Primal Survivor
programme.desc: Hazen Audel embarks on a challenging solo mission to safely deliver 200 reindeer to their spring birthing grounds inside the Arctic Circle. This is an extraordinary overland journey thru the arctic wilderness that Hazen undertakes on foot and pulling a 200lb sled. He leads his herd across frozen rivers, thru blizzards and snow drifts, and over lakes of ice. Will he successfully lead the herd to new pastures? Or mistakenly send them to the bottom of a frozen lake?

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai International, one of the fastest growing airports in the world, is working close to capacity. Delivering ambitious expansion plans while keeping it on track leads to testing times for the staff. Supervisor Mahmoud Alam has a challenge trying to help a Canadian couple bumped off a flight to their dream holiday; David Robson must greet some temperamental VIP passengers; Dispatcher Nizel has a problem when a brand new 389 million dollar A380 goes missing; and time is running out to finish the first phase of the brand new 3.2 billion dollar concourse in just 18 days.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai Airport is nearing full stretch and passenger numbers are growing, so its opening a $3.2 billion dollar extension to Terminal 3 with 20 new departure gates, but not all of it is ready to launch. In Terminal 3, Passenger Operations is coping with a large group of stranded travelers. The Network Control Centre is trying to keep the Emirates fleet on schedule; and a missing passenger and his bags put their curfew-critical flight to Sydney Australia in jeopardy.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: Guarded by the Terracotta Warriors, the tomb of the First Emperor is so revered that it has never been opened. But now, for the first time ever, Sam Willis and his team of experts use new laser drone technology to investigate. What they reveal has never been seen before, and it provides new insight into what lies inside the tomb itself, as they piece together the Emperor's vast mausoleum - an entire, stunning city of the dead.

Its people created amazing Maya-like carvings like nothing ever found in China before. There's also grizzly evidence of human sacrifice on an unprecedented scale. This lost civilisation changes everything we thought we knew about the origins of China, but why, after 500 years, did they vanish? Allan and his team investigate ancient cataclysms to try to solve this great mystery.

In each programme we show 40 to 50 clips of misadventure: ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes and paying the price. We analyse the science behind the mishaps, with the help of bespoke animation and super slow-motion cinematography.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: December 21, 1988. Shortly after taking off from London's Heathrow Airport, Pan Am Flight 103 suddenly explodes in the skies above Lockerbie, Scotland. As the wreckage rains down on the small town below, homes are vaporized and fires break out across the countryside. All 259 people on board are killed. Eleven more in the tiny town also die. The wreckage is scattered over 2,000 square kilometers, and when investigators find proof the aircraft was the victim of a bomb, the area becomes the largest crime scene in the world. Who brought down Flight 103?

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Harrowing stories of tragedy and triumph are brought to life through official reports, transcripts and interviews with the pilots, air traffic controllers and lucky survivors of history's most terrifying crashes. Widely considered to be the safest form of travel, air transportation is still in its infancy and when midair calamity strikes, the results are often catastrophic. From the cockpit to the cabin, from the control room to the crash scene, we uncover just what went wrong.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Harrowing stories of tragedy and triumph are brought to life through official reports, transcripts and interviews with the pilots, air traffic controllers and lucky survivors of history's most terrifying crashes. Widely considered to be the safest form of travel, air transportation is still in its infancy and when midair calamity strikes, the results are often catastrophic. From the cockpit to the cabin, from the control room to the crash scene, we uncover just what went wrong.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: At 1.23AM on April 26th,1986 a massive explosion rips through reactor number four at Chernobyl nuclear power plant in the Soviet Ukraine. A safety test has triggered the worst nuclear accident in history, releasing 400 times more radiation into the atmosphere than the Hiroshima bomb. While control room operators reel in shock, the nuclear plant's firefighters battle the blaze amidst lethal levels of radiation. Just 3km away in the local town of Pripyat, Communist party officials encourage life to go on as normal. Children go to school and couples get married while radiation rains down on them. Meanwhile a giant radioactive cloud starts to circle the globe. Episode 1 follows the first 24-hours of the disaster, with first-hand accounts from people desperately trying to make sense of an accident no one thought could ever happen.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On 28th April, fire still rages in what's left of reactor number 4. Helicopter crews trying to put it out are bombarded by massive doses of radiation. Soviet authorities finally order the evacuation of local residents, and 50,000 people are forced to leave their homes. They are told to take enough for 3 days, but they will never come back. Frontline firefighters suffering acute radiation burns are locked in wards controlled by the KGB. Many will die within days. In Sweden high levels of radiation alert the rest of the world to a radioactive cloud sweeping across Europe - evidence of a massive nuclear accident in the USSR. But the Soviet authorities remain silent. Episode Two charts the first two weeks after the meltdown, revealing the human cost of the accident, and the Soviet efforts to conceal the true scale of the disaster.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Two weeks after the explosion, Soviet authorities race to stop a molten nuclear core from poisoning the water table, and clear deadly radioactive fallout across a 30km exclusion zone. Through the summer of 1986, hundreds of thousands of conscripts and volunteers answer the government's call, to brave lethal radiation levels and build a giant concrete sarcophagus around the destroyed reactor. Meanwhile in Vienna, after months of speculation, the world's nuclear experts gather to hear the Soviet account of what caused the catastrophe. Is Gorbachev's promise of Glasnost finally revealing the truth from behind the Iron Curtain? Or could this be the Soviet's biggest cover up yet? Episode Three uncovers the extraordinary battle to build the most perilous construction project in history told by those who those who were there 40 years ago.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Over a year after the accident, plant operators accused of causing the catastrophe face a Soviet show trial. Inside the power plant, nearly 200 tonnes of lethally radioactive nuclear fuel has gone missing. Scientists hunt for it deep inside the perilous reactor core. As the USSR collapses, an independent investigation searches for evidence of what happened the night of the meltdown, uncovering classified documents that reveal what really caused the explosion, and powerful evidence for a Soviet cover up. Chernobyl's toxic legacy continues right up to the present-day war in Ukraine, when the nuclear exclusion zone turns into a war zone, and the power plant itself comes under attack, raising fears of another nuclear disaster. Episode four reveals how 40-years on, the Chernobyl catastrophe is more relevant than ever.

programme.title: Genoa Bridge Disaster
programme.desc: The special tells the story of the tragic collapse of Morandi's bridge in Genova, in which 43 people lost their lives. The story begins with a chronological re-enactment of the disaster, cut into several scenes displaying critical moments in the unfolding of the collapse. Through the testimonies of the survivors, the show "winds back" the scenes to analyse the causes and events leading up to the tragedy. It ends with the last testimonies of those affected by the catastrophe, followed by a debate on the technological advances made to prevent similar disasters from happening again.

programme.title: AZF: French Factory Explosion
programme.desc: 21 September 2001, 10h17. In the AZF factory, in Toulouse, France, a storage of around 300 tons ammonium nitrate exploded, injuring thousands and killing 31 people. 20 years later, never seen before footage and unpublished interviews show the minute by minute of this industrial disaster, the biggest in French history since WWII.

programme.title: Pole to Pole with Will Smith
programme.desc: Will, Bryan and Carla join local Waorani elder Penti Baihua in the Amazon, in search of the world's biggest snake: the giant green anaconda. They carefully remove just one scale from the 17-foot snake's skin. Will's fear turns into relief, then joy. The single scale can reveal the health of the entire ecosystem — crucial for the future of both wildlife and the Waorani.

programme.title: Pole to Pole with Will Smith
programme.desc: Will travels to the kingdom of Bhutan on a deeply intimate journey in search of the secret to happiness. Guided by happiness expert professor Dacher Keltner and local writer Tshering Denkar, Will treks to one of the highest and happiest villages in the Himalayas, at 13,000-foot altitude. While there, he confronts some of the most challenging moments of his extraordinary life and career.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Air traffic controllers hear an explosion when Air Algérie Flight 6289 lifts off from a remote desert airport. And moments later, the Boeing 737-200 crashes in the Sahara. An intense fire ravages the fuselage, instantly killing all but one of the 103 people on board. For an international team of investigators, it's clear the left engine exploded after liftoff—and that a fatigue crack eventually explains why. What's less obvious is why the highly-experienced crew of the state-owned national carrier was unable to keep their aircraft in the sky. Single-engine failures are infinitely survivable, and the pilots should have been able to recover by working together. Investigators ultimately find their answer in the CVR and in a toxic tug-of-war between a belligerent captain working at cross purposes with Algeria's first-ever female airline pilot.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: January 15th, 2009 - Less than two minutes after taking off from New York's LaGuardia Airport, US Airways Flight #1549 collides with a flock of birds. Both engines suddenly lose all thrust, and the Airbus A-320 begins falling from the sky. The crew desperately tries to restart their engines, but can't. With New York on one side and New Jersey on the other, Captain Chesley Sullenberger is running out of options. Searching desperately for a place to land, Sullenberger warns passengers and crew to brace for impact and tells the control tower that he will be ditching the plane in the icy waters of the Hudson River. Amazingly, all 155 people on board survive, and investigators try to determine how birds could have crippled two of the world's most advanced jet engines. Can they prevent it from happening again?

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: December 21, 1988. Shortly after taking off from London's Heathrow Airport, Pan Am Flight 103 suddenly explodes in the skies above Lockerbie, Scotland. As the wreckage rains down on the small town below, homes are vaporized and fires break out across the countryside. All 259 people on board are killed. Eleven more in the tiny town also die. The wreckage is scattered over 2,000 square kilometers, and when investigators find proof the aircraft was the victim of a bomb, the area becomes the largest crime scene in the world. Who brought down Flight 103?

programme.title: Quest for King Solomon's Treasure
programme.desc: Countless treasure-seekers have set off in search of King Solomon's mines a quest inspired by the Bible's account of spectacular temples adorned in glittering gold and copper. To date, any evidence that supports Solomons existence is regarded as highly controversial. Expeditions in 2010 revealed groundbreaking new clues buried in the Jordanian desert for centuries.

programme.title: Pompeii: Secrets of the Dead
programme.desc: At the height of the Roman Empire, an eruption of Mount Vesuvius buries the town of Pompeii in volcanic ash, killing thousands. Now, forensic experts investigate a group of victims called "the Fugitives" for the very first time. X-rays reveal ages, injuries suffered, and even artefacts like sandals and jewellery - while the investigation also throws light on why they failed to escape the most famous disaster of the ancient world.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: Margot and her canine friends continue their pioneering trapping adventure, pushing deeper into the rugged Yukon backcountry. Desperate for building materials for his island homestead, Matty takes to the seas in search of timber. Billy turns his attention to fixing one of the most essential of all backwoods buildings: the outhouse. Emilie and Gilbert take their daughter Annie on her first hunting trip.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: As temperatures plunge, Margot looks to her dogs to help her seriously raise her wood-harvesting game. Emilie and Gilbert travel to a remote, frozen lake, hoping to net a haul of food for their family. Yearning to bring a little more light into his backwoods life, Billy begins work on an off-grid power system, and Matty heads indoors to complete the latest and perhaps greatest of all his "Hillbilly" building projects.

programme.title: Ice Road Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. Winter storms, avalanches and lack of daylight through the winter season are just some of the challenges they have to face, working close to the polar circle. Extreme winter conditions that often make them risk their lives. "Ice Road Rescue" shows the ultimate man versus nature.

programme.title: Ice Road Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. Winter storms, avalanches and lack of daylight through the winter season are just some of the challenges they have to face, working close to the polar circle. Extreme winter conditions that often make them risk their lives. "Ice Road Rescue" shows the ultimate man versus nature.

programme.title: Ice Road Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. Winter storms, avalanches and lack of daylight through the winter season are just some of the challenges they have to face, working close to the polar circle. Extreme winter conditions that often make them risk their lives. "Ice Road Rescue" shows the ultimate man versus nature.

programme.title: Construction Fails
programme.desc: Florida experienced one of the deadliest structural failures in American history; India's dams are in distress after an avalanche of disasters; a partial collapse shuts down a Mallorca nightclub; and it's a race against time to save Italy's other leaning tower. We find out what happens when air gets into an airplane and how much huffing and puffing it actually takes to bring a brick house down.

programme.title: Construction Fails
programme.desc: When a faraway earthquake brings a Bangkok skyscraper crashing down, investigators wonder why it was the only one to fail. Was a deadly wind whipped up by the tallest tower in Yorkshire? The destruction of a Danish landmark highlights the risks of historic renovation, a Bronx building collapse shows that any renovation is risky if not done according to plan, and working with mobile cranes reveals a long history of contemporary errors.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Air traffic controllers hear an explosion when Air Algérie Flight 6289 lifts off from a remote desert airport. And moments later, the Boeing 737-200 crashes in the Sahara. An intense fire ravages the fuselage, instantly killing all but one of the 103 people on board. For an international team of investigators, it's clear the left engine exploded after liftoff—and that a fatigue crack eventually explains why. What's less obvious is why the highly-experienced crew of the state-owned national carrier was unable to keep their aircraft in the sky. Single-engine failures are infinitely survivable, and the pilots should have been able to recover by working together. Investigators ultimately find their answer in the CVR and in a toxic tug-of-war between a belligerent captain working at cross purposes with Algeria's first-ever female airline pilot.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: January 15th, 2009 - Less than two minutes after taking off from New York's LaGuardia Airport, US Airways Flight #1549 collides with a flock of birds. Both engines suddenly lose all thrust, and the Airbus A-320 begins falling from the sky. The crew desperately tries to restart their engines, but can't. With New York on one side and New Jersey on the other, Captain Chesley Sullenberger is running out of options. Searching desperately for a place to land, Sullenberger warns passengers and crew to brace for impact and tells the control tower that he will be ditching the plane in the icy waters of the Hudson River. Amazingly, all 155 people on board survive, and investigators try to determine how birds could have crippled two of the world's most advanced jet engines. Can they prevent it from happening again?

programme.title: Primal Survivor
programme.desc: Hazen Audel, survival instructor and wilderness guide, travels to the most extreme places on earth to take on solo challenges that will push him to the limit of his knowledge, endurance and skills. Battling dense Guyana jungle, parched Kenyan plains and an active volcano in the Pacific Vanuatu islands. To succeed, he will rely on the ancient techniques of indigenous people who have lived in these environments for thousands of years.

programme.title: Primal Survivor
programme.desc: Hazen Audel embarks on a challenging solo mission to safely deliver 200 reindeer to their spring birthing grounds inside the Arctic Circle. This is an extraordinary overland journey thru the arctic wilderness that Hazen undertakes on foot and pulling a 200lb sled. He leads his herd across frozen rivers, thru blizzards and snow drifts, and over lakes of ice. Will he successfully lead the herd to new pastures? Or mistakenly send them to the bottom of a frozen lake?

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai International is the global commuter's crossroads. Though the airport and airlines try to prepare for every eventuality, more than one million passengers every week inevitably bring regular and unusual challenges. Throw in the uncontrollable - fog, faulty alarms in the brand new concourse, no show passengers - and it's business as usual for staff who have to think on their feet, fast, to keep the airport running on time, and on track.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai International is in the global race to be the world's biggest airport. A massive new concourse for the world's largest fleet of A380's uses over a 150 million man-hours and 930 thousand cubic meters of concrete. Unafraid of new challenges, the airport was the first ever to close two runways back to back for construction. In the words of its Engineering CEO, "the bar is getting higher and higher." There is no room for failure in Dubai.

programme.title: Quest for King Solomon's Treasure
programme.desc: Countless treasure-seekers have set off in search of King Solomon's mines a quest inspired by the Bible's account of spectacular temples adorned in glittering gold and copper. To date, any evidence that supports Solomons existence is regarded as highly controversial. Expeditions in 2010 revealed groundbreaking new clues buried in the Jordanian desert for centuries.

programme.title: Pompeii: Secrets of the Dead
programme.desc: At the height of the Roman Empire, an eruption of Mount Vesuvius buries the town of Pompeii in volcanic ash, killing thousands. Now, forensic experts investigate a group of victims called "the Fugitives" for the very first time. X-rays reveal ages, injuries suffered, and even artefacts like sandals and jewellery - while the investigation also throws light on why they failed to escape the most famous disaster of the ancient world.

programme.title: Wild Vietnam
programme.desc: Travel to southern Vietnam's tropical habitats, home to gibbons and playful macaques, green sea turtles, and Asian black bears. Reintroduced Siamese crocodiles roam the Bau Sau wetlands. The vast Mekong Delta boasts small-clawed otters and thousands of migrating birds, from openbill storks to Oriental darters.

programme.title: Predator v Prey
programme.desc: A forensic look at how Nile crocodiles hunt, offering new perspectives on the patient and precise tactics they use when ambushing migratory herds of wildebeest and zebra that pass through their territories. A male crocodile must target large prey in a bid to survive the harshest time of the year.

programme.title: Wild Vietnam
programme.desc: Travel to Vietnam's mountainous forests to discover endangered pangolins, Asian palm civets, and the golden-headed langurs of Cat Ba Island. The recently discovered Vietnamese crocodile lizard is already vulnerable to smuggling, while the critically endangered Delacour's langurs take refuge in a nature reserve. In springtime, an astonishing diversity of butterfly species congregates in the forest.

programme.title: Return of the Predators
programme.desc: After 30 years of war and destruction, Gorongosa National Park in Mozambique has undergone a remarkable restoration. Herbivores once again bound across the plains, but hunters have been slower to return, leaving the park out of balance...until now!

programme.title: Wild Hunters: Deadly Killers of Africa
programme.desc: Journey across Africa exploring the continent's most iconic and deadliest predators. These African stories are a show of raw power, strength and endurance. An awe-inspiring display of the fierce, everyday battles fought in the wild.

programme.title: Jaguars vs. Crocs
programme.desc: Veteran wildlife photographer Steve Winter and cameraman Bertie Gregory capture the remarkable lives of Jaguars in the Pantanal of Brazil. A mother jaguar teaches her cub survival while a determined male jaguar named Scarface dives headfirst into the river to tackle the deadliest of opponents - 6-foot long caiman. Clever capybaras and playful giant otters stick with their family members to avoid being jaguar dinner.

programme.title: Nicobar Islands: A Monkey's Long Tale
programme.desc: The Nicobar Islands were significantly impacted by the massive tsunami of 2004. This documentary is a compelling exploration of nature's ability to adapt to millions of years of gradual tectonic change and a sudden natural disaster, and a journey into a world few people are fortunate enough to see.

programme.title: Return of the Predators
programme.desc: After 30 years of war and destruction, Gorongosa National Park in Mozambique has undergone a remarkable restoration. Herbivores once again bound across the plains, but hunters have been slower to return, leaving the park out of balance...until now!

programme.title: Wild Hunters: Deadly Killers of Africa
programme.desc: Journey across Africa exploring the continent's most iconic and deadliest predators. These African stories are a show of raw power, strength and endurance. An awe-inspiring display of the fierce, everyday battles fought in the wild.

programme.title: The Incredible Pol Farm
programme.desc: This family farm needs a barn! The Pols are ready to tackle their next big project: preserving and carefully taking down a 100-year-old barn since, with every piece they remove makes the barn weaker and weaker. There is no room for error if they are going to transport these irreplaceable beams back to the farm and restore it to its former glory.

programme.title: The Incredible Pol Farm
programme.desc: The Pols have reached the most dangerous stage of disassembling the barn. If all goes well, they'll transport the beams back to the farm.

programme.title: Lion Brothers: Cubs To Kings
programme.desc: There's one thing that Leo has on his mind from birth - power. And he'll stop at nothing to get it. But life has other ideas and throws the book at this young upstart. With brother Max by his side, can Leo face his challenges and overcome his arrogance to become the King he's always dreamed he'd be?

programme.title: Crocodile - Australia's Greatest Survivor
programme.desc: In Australia, dominant saltwater crocodiles, called ""boss crocs," are the largest and most powerful animals on the continent. Even after unrelenting human pressure on their very survival, they are still the masters of their world. Over the last 200 years, the impact of human activities—such as commercial hunting, agriculture and population growth—pushed them to the brink of extinction. Today, these "boss crocs" have continued to not only survive but thrive.

programme.title: Cat Wars: Lion vs Cheetah
programme.desc: Caught on tape in Africa's wild Serengeti is a stunning act of violence between two top predators. Three cheetahs are brutally attacked by two male lions - the first time an attack like this has ever been filmed. Now, filmmaker Leo Kuenkel, who witnessed the attack, is trying to make sense of it. Was this a once-in-a-lifetime "perfect storm" event? Or do these iconic African cats have a deep-set, sinister reason to fight to the death?

programme.title: Lion Brothers: Cubs To Kings
programme.desc: There's one thing that Leo has on his mind from birth - power. And he'll stop at nothing to get it. But life has other ideas and throws the book at this young upstart. With brother Max by his side, can Leo face his challenges and overcome his arrogance to become the King he's always dreamed he'd be?

programme.title: Crocodile - Australia's Greatest Survivor
programme.desc: In Australia, dominant saltwater crocodiles, called ""boss crocs," are the largest and most powerful animals on the continent. Even after unrelenting human pressure on their very survival, they are still the masters of their world. Over the last 200 years, the impact of human activities—such as commercial hunting, agriculture and population growth—pushed them to the brink of extinction. Today, these "boss crocs" have continued to not only survive but thrive.

programme.title: Reptile Planet
programme.desc: Skittering, swimming and even gliding, over 12,000 species of reptiles roam the world. Crocs, turtles, snakes and lizards have ancestors as old as the dinosaurs and evolution as their friend. These cold-blooded creatures have adapted in clever and lethal ways, from armoured skin and poisonous venom to record-breaking bite force and even blood squirting eyes. This is Reptile Planet!

programme.title: Komodo Dragons
programme.desc: A large adult male is determined to mate with a young female. A trivial episode in the animal kingdom, one might think.

programme.title: Cat Wars: Lion vs Cheetah
programme.desc: Caught on tape in Africa's wild Serengeti is a stunning act of violence between two top predators. Three cheetahs are brutally attacked by two male lions - the first time an attack like this has ever been filmed. Now, filmmaker Leo Kuenkel, who witnessed the attack, is trying to make sense of it. Was this a once-in-a-lifetime "perfect storm" event? Or do these iconic African cats have a deep-set, sinister reason to fight to the death?

programme.title: Diary of A Teen Leopard
programme.desc: Pemba is a Mamma's Boy! At two-years old, he should be independent, but he's still living at home! Like most teenagers, he's impatient and headstrong, which makes him bad at stalking and hunting, so Mom provides all their meals. But when Mom is injured Pemba is forced to grow up fast in order to survive! Can he sever those apron strings and face his fears to launch into adulthood?

programme.title: Tree Climbing Lions
programme.desc: Big cat biologist Alexander Braczkowski is on a mission. He's got 90 days to count lions in Uganda and learn how the population is doing. But these aren't ordinary lions. The prides in Queen Elizabeth National Park spend most of their lives up in the trees, a rare and mysterious behavior unique to this part of Africa.

programme.title: Mashatu: Land of Leopards
programme.desc: In Botswana's Mashatu Game Reserve, one of the last sanctuaries for free-roaming leopards, a mother gives birth to two cubs. She'll stop at nothing to protect them. But as climate change tightens its grip, resources dwindle, and a fierce leopard war looms. Over three years of intimate observation, this film unveils rare, breathtaking footage of behaviors never before documented, offering an unprecedented glimpse into the resilience and secrets of this elusive species.

programme.title: Lion Brothers: Cubs To Kings
programme.desc: There's one thing that Leo has on his mind from birth - power. And he'll stop at nothing to get it. But life has other ideas and throws the book at this young upstart. With brother Max by his side, can Leo face his challenges and overcome his arrogance to become the King he's always dreamed he'd be?

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol
programme.desc: With more than 19,000 patients, Dr. Pol has seen it all. Specializing in large farm animals, this senior is anything but retiring as he takes an old-school, non-nonsense approach to veterinary medicine. Unflappable and unstoppable, this doc routinely puts in 14-hour days and is a legend in his community. This new series travels with him across rural Michigan to care for every family pet and head of livestock in need of his expertise and kindness.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol
programme.desc: With more than 19,000 patients, Dr. Pol has seen it all. Specializing in large farm animals, this senior is anything but retiring as he takes an old-school, non-nonsense approach to veterinary medicine. Unflappable and unstoppable, this doc routinely puts in 14-hour days and is a legend in his community. This new series travels with him across rural Michigan to care for every family pet and head of livestock in need of his expertise and kindness.

programme.title: Killer Safari
programme.desc: Life in the African bush is a game of survival, for both hunter and hunted. Add babies to the mix and you'll see astounding feats of courage as mothers risk life and limb to protect their young. In spring, tall grass helps babies hide out of sight, but it also camouflages predators on the prowl. This game drive captures extraordinary behaviour as families and individuals battle to eat, defend, and raise young.

programme.title: Killer Safari
programme.desc: The lines between hunter and hunted become blurred in this epic safari. Hold on to your seats as an elephant charges through the bush with a lion dangling from its tusks. Watch as male leopards engage in a turf war, and a pack of hyenas bully a lion off of his buffalo kill. The safari intensifies as a leopard, wild dogs and hyenas fight for the same kill in an astonishing display of raw nature.

programme.title: Lion Brothers: Cubs To Kings
programme.desc: There's one thing that Leo has on his mind from birth - power. And he'll stop at nothing to get it. But life has other ideas and throws the book at this young upstart. These cold-blooded creatures have adapted in clever and lethal ways, from armoured skin and poisonous venom to record-breaking bite force and even blood squirting eyes. This is Reptile Planet!

programme.title: The Incredible Pol Farm
programme.desc: The Pol's new livestock aren't potty trained; their sheep hut isn't draining properly and has now been transformed into a Pee Palace. It's late fall and the winter deep freeze is just around the corner, so the family needs to get it fixed fast. The Pol's also get a new sheep for their farm.

programme.title: The Incredible Pol Farm
programme.desc: The Pol clan gets to work on creating Beth's dream garden. To beat the incoming rainstorm, an exhausted Ben works around the clock to finish building the garden's pond and waterfall. Noticing her brother is near his breaking point, Beth plans a special surprise to lift his spirits.

programme.title: Wild Taiwan: Jungle Island
programme.desc: Explores the rural nature and exotic animals that live on Jungle Island.

programme.title: Austria's Wild Heritage - One Country Six National Parks
programme.desc: The second part focuses on the challenges of the future: How do the national parks deal with invasive species from outside and what success stories can be reported? For instance, in the Hohe Tauern National Park the previously extinct ibexes and bearded vultures have been reintroduced. In the Gesäuse National Park Avalanches created one of the most species-rich habitats, which rare butterflies like the Red Apollo colonize.

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: In Oman's ancient seas and deserts, life is abundant yet challenging for its diverse wildlife. Journey with local scientists through landscapes adorned with frankincense trees, horned vipers, and tail-lashing lizards. Witness beach patrols with turtles and explore caves hosting unique fish. Along the Arabian coast, spinner dolphins, whales, and turtles enrich Oman's vibrant marine life.

programme.title: Wild Taiwan: Jungle Island
programme.desc: Explores the rural nature and exotic animals that live on Jungle Island.

programme.title: Austria's Wild Heritage - One Country Six National Parks
programme.desc: The second part focuses on the challenges of the future: How do the national parks deal with invasive species from outside and what success stories can be reported? For instance, in the Hohe Tauern National Park the previously extinct ibexes and bearded vultures have been reintroduced. In the Gesäuse National Park Avalanches created one of the most species-rich habitats, which rare butterflies like the Red Apollo colonize.

programme.title: Winter's Hidden Wonders
programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

programme.title: Winter's Hidden Wonders
programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: In Oman's ancient seas and deserts, life is abundant yet challenging for its diverse wildlife. Journey with local scientists through landscapes adorned with frankincense trees, horned vipers, and tail-lashing lizards. Witness beach patrols with turtles and explore caves hosting unique fish. Along the Arabian coast, spinner dolphins, whales, and turtles enrich Oman's vibrant marine life.

programme.title: Wild Arabia
programme.desc: Discover Saudi Arabia's rugged landscapes where wildlife ingeniously adapts to extreme conditions. Amid sun-scorched terrain, creatures endure searing sun, thin air, and toxic gases. Explore bone caves, and marvel at rock art depicting local fauna—deadly scorpions, raucous baboons, charming chameleons, and endangered leopards. Their resilience mirrors the harsh beauty of Saudi Arabia's environment.

programme.title: Untamed Philippines
programme.desc: Moving from turtles of the Palawan coast to the unique National Marine Park at Tubbataha, to meet giants, whale sharks and manta rays. We also see how people can mediate between people and wildlife as we join a snake hunting team catching and translocating a deadly Samar cobra.

programme.title: Untamed Philippines
programme.desc: We start on the tiny island of Batan, meeting a unique viper and a ubiquitous but also giant, land crab. Luzon is the main island of the Philippines. We drop in to Mindoro to see the most endangered large mammal in the Philippines, the tamaraw.

programme.title: Wild Taiwan: Jungle Island
programme.desc: Explores the rural nature and exotic animals that live on Jungle Island.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol

programme.title: Great White vs Tiger Shark
programme.desc: The two biggest of the BIG THREE most dangerous sharks - great whites and tiger sharks. And they get BIG - up to 20 feet and 18 feet respectively. But where can we find the world's biggest? And what is it like to meet these massive creatures face-to-face? Join two teams of shark experts on encounters with some of the largest sharks ever caught on camera, including the famed great white Deep Blue.

programme.title: World's Biggest Great White?
programme.desc: Three experienced divers and photographers set out on a small boat to try to record any marine life around the carcass of a sperm whale floating off the coast of Oahu. What they experienced is one of the most incredible great white shark encounters ever caught on camera.

programme.title: Wild Taiwan: Jungle Island
programme.desc: Explores the rural nature and exotic animals that live on Jungle Island.
